name: Crepes Suzette
recipeIngredient: 3 dl Milk, boiled and cooled (5/8 pint)
recipeInstructions:
1. THE BATTER: Combine flour, sugar, and salt in a bowl. Whisk in eggs one at a time with a wooden spatula.
2. Add milk gradually and mix until smooth and homogeneous. Stir in cream, remaining milk, and curagao. Cover the bowl with a plate and let stand at room temperature for at least 1 hour before cooking crepes.
3. COOKING THE CREPES: Brush pan with clarified butter and heat. Pour a small amount of batter into the center of the pan, tilt to spread thinly over the base. Cook until set on one side (about 1.5 minutes), then turn over with a palette knife and cook for another 1.5 minutes until golden on both sides. Place crepe on a plate and repeat to make 18 crepes.
4. THE SAUCE: Crush sugar lumps against orange skin to absorb flavor. Whisk softened butter and sugar together, add curagao and sugar lumps. SERVING: Squeeze orange juice into a pan over high heat until reduced by half. Return the pan to heat and beat the butter mixture into the orange juice, bringing it to a boil. Lift crepes one at a time with a fork and spread in the sauce, turning over and folding into quarters. Repeat with all crepes.
